title: Snow Phenology Sentinel-2 & Sentinel-1 2016-present (raster 60 m, LAEA projection), Europe, annually
description: The Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 Snow Phenology (SP S1+ S2) product is generated once a year over high-mountain areas at European scale, based on daily cumulative Gap-Filled Fractional Snow Cover (GFSC) products calculated from Sentinel-2 optical and Sentinel-1 radar data. This product describes the snow season in terms of temporality as it provides, within an hydrological year and for each pixel, the number of days with snow cover, as well as the first and the last day of the longest observed snow period. The hydrological year starts from 1st September. The native product has a spatial resolution of 60 m x 60 m, as does the input GFSC product. This product corresponds to a reprojection of the native product to the LAEA (Lambert azimuthal equal-area projection) grid.

links.title: Legal notice on the use of Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) data
Homepage of the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service

cdse-s3.title: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem S3
cdse-s3.description: This endpoint provides access to EO data which is stored on the object storage of both CloudFerro Cloud and OpenTelekom Cloud (OTC). See the [documentation](https://documentation.dataspace.copernicus.eu/APIs/S3.html) for more information, including how to get credentials.

creodias-s3.title: CREODIAS S3
creodias-s3.description: Comprehensive Earth Observation Data (EODATA) archive offered by CREODIAS as a commercial part of CDSE, designed to provide users with access to a vast repository of satellite data without predefined quota limits

QAFLAGS.title: Quality Flags (QAFLAGS)
QAFLAGS.description: Bitmask layer with pixel quality flags

classification:bitfields.name: snow_cover_duration_too_low
classification:bitfields.description: Indicates pixels with no sufficient snowfall throughout the year to allow for an accurate estimate of the snow season.

classes.name: not_snow_cover_duration_too_low
classes.description: The pixel receives sufficient snowfall throughout the year to characterize the snow season.

classes.name: snow_cover_duration_too_low
classes.description: The pixel does not receive sufficient snowfall throughout the year to allow for an accurate estimate of the snow season.

classification:bitfields.name: tree_cover_duration_too_high
classification:bitfields.description: Indicates whether the tree cover density is too high for an accurate snow detection.

classes.name: not_tree_cover_duration_too_high
classes.description: The tree cover density is not too high for an accurate snow detection.

classes.name: tree_cover_duration_too_high
classes.description: The tree cover density is too high for an accurate snow detection.

classification:bitfields.name: wet_snow_detection_area
classification:bitfields.description: Indicates whether the pixel is part of the area in which wet snow detection is applied (high elevation, non-forested, non-urban, non-water areas).

classes.name: not_wet_snow_detection_area
classes.description: The pixel is not part of the area in which wet snow detection is applied.

classes.name: wet_snow_detection_area
classes.description: The pixel is part of the area in which wet snow detection is applied.

SCD.title: Snow Cover Duration (days)
SCD.description: Number of days of snow cover over the hydrological year (between 0 and 366)

SCO.title: Snow Cover Onset-date (day of the year)
SCO.description: Snow cover onset-date defined as the first day of the longest snow cover period. Date expressed as the number of days from the beginning of the hydrological year (1st September = day 0).

SCM.title: Snow Cover Melt out date (day of the year)
SCM.description: Snow cover melt out date defined as the last day of the longest snow cover period. Date expressed as the number of days from the beginning of the hydrological year (1st September = day 0).

NCSO.title: Number of days with clear sky observations over the hydrological year used in the interpolation of the snow cover time series.
NCSO.description: Number of days with clear sky observations used in the interpolation of the snow cover time series (between 0 and 366).

NWSO.title: Number of days with S1-based wet snow observations over the hydrological year used in the interpolation of the snow cover time series
NWSO.description: Number of days with S1-based wet snow observations used in the interpolation of the snow cover time series (used where S2-based observations have gaps) (between 0 and 366).

SCD-QA.title: Quality layer for the Snow Cover Duration (SCD) layer
SCD-QA.description: Quality layer providing basic assessment of the Snow Cover Duration layer.

SCO-QA.title: Quality indicator associated with the Snow Cover Onset (SCO) date
SCO-QA.description: Number of days between the estimated snow onset date and the closest snow observation date (between 0 and 366).

SCM-QA.title: Quality indicator associated with the Snow Cover Melt out (SCM) date
SCM-QA.description: Number of days between the estimated snow melt out date and the closest snow observation date (between 0 and 366).

SCD-MTD.title: SCD auxiliary metadata
SCD-MTD.description: GDAL auxiliary metadata associated with the SCD raster layer (color table)

SCO-MTD.title: SCDSCO auxiliary metadata
SCO-MTD.description: GDAL auxiliary metadata associated with the SCO raster layer (color table)

SCM-MTD.title: SCM auxiliary metadata
SCM-MTD.description: GDAL auxiliary metadata associated with the SCM raster layer (color table)

NCSO-MTD.title: NCSO auxiliary metadata
NCSO-MTD.description: GDAL auxiliary metadata associated with the NCSO raster layer (color table)

NWSO-MTD.title: NWSO auxiliary metadata
NWSO-MTD.description: GDAL auxiliary metadata associated with the NWSO raster layer (color table)

SCD-QA-MTD.title: SCD-QA auxiliary metadata
SCD-QA-MTD.description: GDAL auxiliary metadata associated with the SCD-QA raster layer (color table)
